title: Impostor Syndrome Quiz
description: Find out your impostor syndrome level — spoiler: even 10x engineers feel it
viralHook: I got 'Senior Impostor' on this quiz and I've been coding for 8 years 😭

props.title: How Bad Is Your Impostor Syndrome?

questions.question: You just shipped a big feature. How do you feel?
options: I crushed it, obviously
Relieved nobody found the bugs yet
Waiting for someone to realize I copy-pasted from Stack Overflow
Already dreading the next one
questions.explanation: If you shipped it, you earned it. Even if Stack Overflow helped.

questions.question: A junior dev asks you to explain a concept. You:
options: Explain it confidently — I know this
Explain it while internally panicking I'll get it wrong
Google it first 'just to make sure'
Redirect them to the docs and pray
questions.explanation: Teaching is the best way to learn. If you can explain it, you know it.

questions.question: You're in a code review and someone suggests changes:
options: Great feedback, let me improve this
They found out I'm a fraud
I should have caught that — I'm terrible
Time to update my LinkedIn to 'seeking opportunities'
questions.explanation: Code reviews improve code, not judge you. Everyone gets feedback.

questions.question: Your company announces a new tech stack. You:
options: Excited to learn something new
Nervous but I'll figure it out
Convinced everyone else already knows it
Start polishing my resume
questions.explanation: Nobody knows everything. The best engineers are the ones who keep learning.

questions.question: You solve a really hard bug. Your reaction:
options: I'm good at this
That was mostly luck
Someone else would have found it faster
I hope nobody asks me to explain how I fixed it
questions.explanation: Luck is when preparation meets opportunity. You earned that fix.

questions.question: Someone calls you a 'senior developer'. You think:
options: Yeah, I've put in the work
They must not have seen my git history
There must be a mistake on my title
I'm just a junior who's been here too long
questions.explanation: Titles reflect your contributions. Own it.
